message:
feat: writer partial-flush resilience, path sanitization, and session-aware reports

Three improvements across the telemetry writer and report generation:

1. writer.rs — Partial-flush failure resilience: when a flush fails
   partway through, unwritten batches are now kept in the buffer and
   row counts remain accurate instead of silently discarding data.
   Drop implementation skips the atomic rename and deletes the temp
   file if finalization failed, preventing corrupt output files.
   Path sanitization: new sanitize_path_component helper strips
   path-traversal characters (/, .., \) from host_id and session_id
   before embedding in output paths.  Process ID added to output
   filenames for additional uniqueness.  Three new tests cover
   flush-failure buffering, drop-after-failure cleanup, and path
   sanitization.

2. session/mod.rs — Adds read_context method to SessionHandle that
   deserializes context.json via serde_json, mirroring the existing
   write_context.  Existing test renamed and expanded to cover the
   round-trip.

3. main.rs — generate_report_from_session now loads the optional
   context.json and uses host_id, os.family, and os.arch in the
   report overview instead of placeholder values.  Falls back to
   "unknown" when the file is absent.  Three inline tests verify
   with-context, without-context, and invalid-JSON error cases.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
